brief: Global economic trends and diplomatic developments are intersecting in complex ways, with the US planning to restore Hong Kong's special trading status, potentially easing trade restrictions and marking a shift in US-China relations. Meanwhile, the global economy is tipping towards a sharp slowdown, according to Moody's Analytics, which could impact India's growth, even as the country's startup ecosystem may indirectly benefit from the upcoming IPOs of AI companies. In India, economic indicators such as inflation numbers and bank credit growth are being closely watched, with the RBI's policy decisions likely to have significant implications for the country's macroeconomic outlook.

Geopolitics.title: Controversy in Venice: A Diplomatic Voyage and Public Protest
Geopolitics.summary: The US Ambassador to Italy's visit to Venice on his yacht sparked protests against Trump's policies and criticism over mass tourism. The visit coincided with a local festival, Festa del Redentore, and highlighted international political tensions. This incident reflects the complexities of diplomatic visits and their potential impact on local communities.

Geopolitics.title: Revival of Hong Kong's Special Trade Status Marks Positive Shift in US-China Relations
Geopolitics.summary: The US plans to restore Hong Kong's special trading status, which was revoked in 2020 due to China's national security law. This decision follows recent trade talks between the US and China, potentially easing trade restrictions and marking a shift in their international relations. The move could have significant implications for global trade and US-China relations.

Geopolitics.title: Kenya airport deal proves China’s disinformation drive to win African infra projects
Geopolitics.summary: China has been using disinformation and debt diplomacy to secure infrastructure projects in Africa, as seen in the Kenya airport deal. This strategy involves using media influence and psychological operations to win contracts, often at inflated prices. The practice has significant implications for African countries and their economic development.

Geopolitics.title: India’s BRICS presidency is championing women-led diplomacy
Geopolitics.summary: India's presidency of the BRICS grouping is promoting women-led diplomacy, a significant development in international relations. This approach aims to increase the participation of women in diplomatic efforts and promote gender equality. The initiative reflects India's commitment to promoting diversity and inclusion in global governance.

Geopolitics.title: 125 Nations Observe July 17 As World Day for International Justice
Geopolitics.summary: July 17 is observed as World Day for International Justice, marking the establishment of the International Criminal Court (ICC). The ICC was set up to prosecute global crimes such as genocides, war crimes, and crimes against humanity. The day highlights the importance of international justice and the need to end impunity for such crimes.

Economy.title: Why India Needs Three Inflation Numbers To Understand One Economy
Economy.summary: India uses three inflation numbers - CPI, WPI, and PPI - to measure inflation at different stages of the economy, which helps in understanding price trends, corporate margins, and the RBI's policy decisions. These numbers provide a comprehensive view of the economy and are essential for policymakers. They also help in predicting India's growth outlook.

Economy.title: Crisil sees volatile FY27: Inflation to rise to 5.1%, growth to slow; RBI may hike rates
Economy.summary: According to a Crisil report, India's economy is expected to face challenges in 2026-27 due to rising inflation and slowing growth, despite easing geopolitical tensions and lower crude oil prices. The RBI may respond to this by hiking interest rates. This could impact the country's macroeconomic outlook.

Economy.title: Global economy tipping towards a sharp slowdown, India ‘may lose a step’ too: Moody's Analytics
Economy.summary: Moody's Analytics has warned that the global economy is headed for a slowdown, which could also impact India's growth. The slowdown is expected to be triggered by an extended US-Iran conflict, leading to a fresh energy shock, inflation, and tighter monetary policy. This could force central banks to take cautious measures.

Economy.title: Former RBI governor Raghuram Rajan, 2 other Indians appointed in key US Federal Reserve task forces
Economy.summary: Raghuram Rajan, former RBI governor, and two other Indians have been appointed to key US Federal Reserve task forces. They will review key areas of US monetary policy, providing their expertise to shape the future of US monetary policymaking. This is a significant development, as it highlights the recognition of Indian experts in global economic matters.

Business.title: AI IPO wave: Why India's start-up ecosystem could be an indirect winner
Business.summary: The upcoming IPOs of OpenAI, Anthropic, and SpaceX may indirectly benefit India's startup ecosystem by reviving VC funding, improving exits, and boosting deep-tech investment. This could strengthen India's long-term AI ambitions and reshape global venture capital flows. Successful AI listings may have a positive impact on India's startup ecosystem.

Business.title: Bank credit growth to ease as corporates tap bond markets, overseas funding
Business.summary: Bank credit growth is expected to moderate as large corporations increasingly use domestic bond markets and overseas borrowings for funding. The Reserve Bank of India's policy measures make external funding more attractive for businesses, reducing reliance on traditional bank loans. Overall credit demand remains healthy, but banks will finance a smaller share.

Business.title: Neuberg readies for IPO in FY28, to seek up to Rs 4,000 cr
Business.summary: Neuberg Diagnostics plans an initial public offering in fiscal year 2028, aiming to raise approximately ₹3,500-4,000 crore. The company will use a pre-IPO funding round to secure Rs 5000 crore for acquisitions, targeting pan-India growth and new diagnostic technologies. Neuberg expects to become the second-largest player in the diagnostics market.

Business.title: Corporate Paper issuances touch 5-yr high at Rs 2.53 lakh crore in June
Business.summary: Indian companies raised Rs 2.53 lakh crore via commercial papers in June, a near five-year high. This surge was driven by attractive pricing, seasonal needs, and debt refinancing, with experts noting a shift from costlier bank loans to cheaper short-term market funding. The Reserve Bank of India's liquidity infusions also contributed to favorable conditions.

National Affairs.title: Voter list name deletion does not mean automatic loss of citizenship
National Affairs.summary: The Supreme Court has clarified that being deleted from the electoral roll does not automatically mean a person loses their citizenship status. This is because the Election Commission of India is not the final authority to determine citizenship. The status remains for other purposes until a final decision is made.

National Affairs.title: 'You ignored because she was poor?'
National Affairs.summary: The Supreme Court has criticized a doctor and hospital for denying medical care to a 4-year-old rape victim, questioning their empathy and actions. The court's comments highlight concerns about access to medical care, particularly for vulnerable populations. The case has sparked a wider discussion about healthcare and social responsibility.

National Affairs.title: Ram Mandir Donation Row: SIT Seeks More Time To File Report, May Submit It In Supreme Court
National Affairs.summary: A Special Investigation Team in Uttar Pradesh is probing alleged embezzlement of donations at Ayodhya's Ram Temple and has requested more time to finalize its investigation. The team may submit its report to the Supreme Court. The case involves allegations of financial irregularities and corruption.

National Affairs.summary: The Supreme Court has reprimanded two private hospitals and their doctors for failing to provide timely medical care to a 4-year-old rape-and-murder victim, emphasizing the importance of doctors fulfilling their duties.

National Affairs.title: 'Exclusion Or Deletion From Voter List Under SIR Not Loss Of Citizenship': Supreme Court
National Affairs.summary: The Supreme Court has clarified that being removed from electoral rolls under the Special Intensive Revision (SIR) does not affect a person's citizenship status, and has raised concerns about individuals being denied welfare benefits due to this misconception.

National Affairs.title: SC extends deadline for conducting elections of 5 city corporations in Bengaluru
National Affairs.summary: The Supreme Court has extended the deadline for conducting elections in five city corporations in Bengaluru to December 2026, citing the ongoing Special Intensive Revision (SIR) of electoral rolls.

National Affairs.title: Ram Mandir Donation Row: SIT Seeks More Time To File Report, May Submit It In Supreme Court

Business.title: Aukera raises Rs 90 crore in fresh funding
Business.summary: Aukera, a premium lab-grown diamond jewellery brand, has raised Rs 90 crore in fresh funding from investors including Alteria Capital and InnoVen Capital. This funding round comes less than a year after the company raised $15 million in equity funding. The investment will help Aukera accelerate its national expansion plans.

The issue revolves around the administration of religious institutions and the state government's role in it. The case has sparked debate about the secular character of India and the influence of the Sangh Parivar agenda.

National Affairs.title: SC allows release of animated film 'Mahaprabhu Jagannath' post 'Lord Jagannath Yatra'
National Affairs.summary: The Supreme Court has permitted the all-India release of the animated film 'Mahaprabhu Jagannath' after the Lord Jagannath Yatra festivities. The film's release was allowed as it is based on a web series already available on YouTube. The court's decision comes after considering the film's content and its potential impact.

National Affairs.title: UP Government's Bulldozer Action Against Jauhar University Sparks Controversy
National Affairs.summary: The Uttar Pradesh government's decision to demolish Mohammad Ali Jauhar University has sparked controversy, with critics accusing it of communal politics and contempt of the Supreme Court. The university plans to challenge the demolition order in court, raising questions about the legal due process. The issue has been criticized by Bhim Army leader Chandra Shekhar Azad.

National Affairs.title: SC acts after courtroom chaos, tightens rules for litigants-in-person
National Affairs.summary: The Supreme Court has tightened rules for litigants-in-person after a recent incident of courtroom chaos. The decision was made at a full court meeting of all Supreme Court judges and aims to maintain order and discipline in the court. The new rules will regulate the behavior of litigants who choose to represent themselves in court.

National Affairs.title: 1994 murder case: SC questions Bihar govt’s move to grant remission to former MP Anand Mohan
National Affairs.summary: The Supreme Court has questioned the Bihar government's decision to grant remission to former MP Anand Mohan, who was convicted of murder in 1994. The court observed that the decision seemed to be biased in favor of the convict, with all factors working in his favor to secure release. The case has raised concerns about the fairness of the remission process.

National Affairs.summary: The Supreme Court expressed concerns that introducing a third language in schools from Class IX may cause undue stress on students. National Affairs.title: PM Modi should break his silence over 'embezzlement' of Ram temple donations: Congress
National Affairs.summary: The Congress party has urged Prime Minister Narendra Modi to address allegations of embezzlement of donations at the Ram temple in Ayodhya. The party demands a Supreme Court-monitored probe into the matter.

National Affairs.title: Omar Abdullah claims BJP offered his MLA ₹20-30 crore, ministerial berth, J&K ‘statehood’ to switch sides
National Affairs.summary: Omar Abdullah has alleged that a BJP official offered his MLA a large sum of money, a ministerial position, and statehood for Jammu and Kashmir in exchange for switching parties. The MLA reportedly rejected the offer.

brief: Tensions are escalating globally, with Iran launching missile strikes on its Gulf neighbours and Russia intensifying its attacks on Ukraine, drawing in the US and posing significant risks to regional security and international diplomacy. Meanwhile, in India, the government is focusing on strengthening its position in the Strait of Malacca through the Great Nicobar project, while also emphasizing the importance of personal rapport and trust in diplomacy, as seen in Prime Minister Narendra Modi's recent three-nation tour. On the economic front, the Reserve Bank of India is expected to keep policy rates steady in the short term, but may increase rates later this year due to rising domestic inflation concerns, which could impact India's growth forecast, already revised downwards to 6.6% by the Asian Development Bank.

Geopolitics.title: Iran strikes back: Missiles rain down on Gulf neighbours
Geopolitics.summary: Iran has launched missile and drone strikes on its Gulf neighbours in response to an attack on a merchant vessel, escalating tensions in the region and drawing in the US. The Strait of Hormuz has been closed, impacting international shipping routes and oil exports. This development has significant implications for regional security and global diplomacy.

Geopolitics.title: Escalating Conflict: Ukraine Struggles Amid Russian Assaults
Geopolitics.summary: Russia has intensified its attacks on Ukraine, resulting in multiple casualties and highlighting the need for faster weapon shipments from allies. The conflict is escalating, posing significant risks to civilian safety and international diplomatic relations. Ukrainian President Zelenskiy has emphasized the urgency of the situation.

Geopolitics.title: Great Nicobar project to solidify India’s position in Strait of Malacca: Report
Geopolitics.summary: The Great Nicobar project is expected to strengthen India's position in the Strait of Malacca, a critical shipping route. The project's significance has increased in the context of the Iran war and its potential impact on regional geopolitics. It may enhance India's influence in the region and secure its interests.

Geopolitics.title: Global rights group revives focus on religious persecution of minorities during 1971 Bangladesh Liberation War
Geopolitics.summary: A global rights group has renewed attention on the religious persecution of minorities during the 1971 Bangladesh Liberation War. The war resulted in mass killings and displacement, and this development may lead to a re-examination of the conflict's legacy and its ongoing impact on regional relations. It highlights the importance of addressing historical injustices and promoting human rights.

Geopolitics.title: nation tour: Diplomacy built on personal rapport and trust
Geopolitics.summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi's recent three-nation tour has highlighted the importance of personal rapport and trust in diplomacy. The tour may have strengthened India's relationships with the visited countries, and its outcomes will be closely watched for their impact on regional and global geopolitics. The emphasis on personal connections in diplomacy can lead to more effective cooperation and conflict resolution.

Economy.title: E20 petrol may reduce mileage by 3-5%, says Oil Ministry
Economy.summary: The Oil Ministry has stated that E20 petrol, which contains 20% ethanol, may reduce vehicle mileage by 3-5%. However, it offers benefits like cleaner engines and lower carbon emissions. This information is crucial for consumers and automobile manufacturers as India plans to roll out E20 petrol.

Economy.title: RBI rate hike: Interest burden for Indians may go up later this year amid inflation risks
Economy.summary: The Reserve Bank of India is expected to keep policy rates steady in the short term, but may increase rates later this year due to rising domestic inflation concerns. This could lead to a higher interest burden for Indians. The economic growth projections for FY27 have been revised, indicating a stronger demand trend.

Economy.title: Why India is launching an index of services production
Economy.summary: India is launching an index of services production to provide high-frequency information on the services economy, which will help in making better policy decisions and economic forecasting. This index will complement the existing index of industrial production. The services sector is a significant contributor to India's economy, and this index will help in understanding its trends and patterns.

Economy.title: ADB cuts India FY27 growth forecast to 6.6% on higher oil prices
Economy.summary: The Asian Development Bank has lowered India's FY27 growth forecast to 6.6% due to higher crude oil prices and transportation costs, which are expected to impact consumer sentiment and demand. The bank has retained its FY28 growth forecast at 7.3%, expecting global conditions to improve. The inflation forecast for FY27 has been raised to 5.2% due to oil and food prices.

Business.title: Aukera Raises Rs. 90 Cr in Fresh Funding
Business.summary: Aukera, a premium lab-grown diamond jewellery brand, has raised Rs. 90 crore in fresh funding to accelerate its national expansion. This funding comes less than a year after the company raised USD 15 million, reflecting the strength of its growth trajectory. The investment will help Aukera strengthen its balance sheet and maintain its lead in the market.

Business.title: Bank credit growth to ease as corporates tap bond markets, overseas funding
Business.summary: Bank credit growth is expected to slow down as large corporations start using domestic bond markets and overseas borrowings for funding. This shift is driven by the Reserve Bank of India's policy measures, which make external funding more attractive for businesses. As a result, banks will finance a smaller share of corporate financing needs.

Business.title: Neuberg readies for IPO in FY28, to seek up to Rs 4,000 cr
Business.summary: Neuberg Diagnostics plans to launch an initial public offering in fiscal year 2028, aiming to raise up to Rs 4,000 crore. The company will use the funds to drive pan-India growth and invest in new diagnostic technologies. A pre-IPO funding round will also be used to secure Rs 500 crore for acquisitions.

Business.title: Corporate Paper issuances touch 5-yr high at Rs 2.53 lakh crore in June
Business.summary: Indian companies raised a record Rs 2.53 lakh crore through commercial papers in June, driven by attractive pricing and seasonal needs. This surge reflects a shift from costlier bank loans to cheaper short-term market funding, supported by ample investor demand and a stable liquidity environment. The Reserve Bank of India's liquidity infusions also contributed to the favorable conditions.

Business.title: India's banks are handing out more loans, but deposits aren't keeping up
Business.summary: Indian banks have seen a surge in lending, outpacing deposit growth and widening the funding gap. This trend has pushed the loan-to-deposit ratio to a decade-high, raising concerns about funding sustainability. The mismatch between loan growth and deposit growth reflects varied strategies amidst intense competition in the banking sector.

Business.title: Almost half of UDAN routes have become non-operational
Business.summary: Nearly half of India's UDAN regional flight routes have become non-operational, despite the government launching a revamped phase with higher subsidies and airport funding. The viability gap support, infrastructure delays, and airline distress are key factors contributing to this situation. The development has significant implications for the country's flagship connectivity scheme.

National Affairs.title: OFCD case: SC to hear SEBI's plea against SAT relief to SICCL managers on Monday
National Affairs.summary: The Supreme Court will hear SEBI's plea challenging the Securities Appellate Tribunal's order granting relief to Sahara India Commercial Corporation Ltd managers. The case is significant as it involves the regulation of financial markets and the powers of regulatory bodies. The outcome may have implications for corporate governance and regulatory frameworks in India.

National Affairs.title: The Goondas Act: Is India's reliance on preventive detention undermining rule of law?
National Affairs.summary: The use of preventive detention in India has raised concerns about the rule of law and democratic principles. Despite Supreme Court warnings, governments continue to use preventive detention, sparking debates about its implications for individual rights and the justice system. This issue highlights the tension between national security and individual freedoms.

National Affairs.title: Right of way: The right to walk safely demands more than urban design standards for demarcated pavements
National Affairs.summary: The Supreme Court has recognized walking on demarcated footpaths as a fundamental right, emphasizing the need for safe and accessible pedestrian infrastructure. This ruling has implications for urban planning and design, highlighting the importance of prioritizing pedestrian safety and accessibility in Indian cities. It also underscores the role of urban design in promoting public health and well-being.

National Affairs.title: PFI Terror Conspiracy case: Delhi Court puts PFI leader on trial for waging war against India, conspiracy for overthrowing govt
National Affairs.summary: A Delhi court has charged a Popular Front of India leader with waging war against India and conspiring to overthrow the government. The case involves allegations of terrorism and anti-national activities, and the trial's outcome may have significant implications for national security and the regulation of extremist groups in India. The charges are based on evidence gathered by the NIA.

National Affairs.title: Omar Abdullah claims BJP offered his MLA ₹20-30 crore, ministerial berth, J&K ‘statehood’ to switch sides
National Affairs.summary: Omar Abdullah has alleged that the BJP offered one of his MLAs a large sum of money, a ministerial position, and the promise of Jammu and Kashmir's statehood in exchange for switching parties. This claim highlights the ongoing political dynamics in Jammu and Kashmir and the tensions between different political parties in the region. The allegation also raises questions about political integrity and the use of incentives to influence political loyalty.

This funding comes less than a year after the company raised USD 15 million, reflecting the strength of its growth trajectory. The investment will help Aukera strengthen its balance sheet as it competes with larger players in the market.

Business.title: Bank credit growth to ease as corporates tap bond markets, overseas funding
Business.summary: Bank credit growth is expected to slow down as large corporations opt for domestic bond markets and overseas borrowings for funding. This shift is driven by the Reserve Bank of India's policy measures, which make external funding more attractive for businesses. As a result, banks will finance a smaller share of corporate financing needs.

Business.title: Neuberg readies for IPO in FY28, to seek up to Rs 4,000 cr
Business.summary: Neuberg Diagnostics plans to launch an initial public offering in fiscal year 2028, aiming to raise up to Rs 4,000 crore. The company will use the funds to drive pan-India growth and acquire new diagnostic technologies, with the goal of becoming the second-largest player in the diagnostics market. A pre-IPO funding round will also be undertaken to secure Rs 500 crore for acquisitions.

Business.title: Corporate Paper issuances touch 5-yr high at Rs 2.53 lakh crore in June
Business.summary: Indian companies raised a record Rs 2.53 lakh crore through commercial papers in June, driven by attractive pricing, seasonal needs, and debt refinancing. This surge reflects a shift from costlier bank loans to cheaper short-term market funding, supported by ample investor demand and a stable liquidity environment. The Reserve Bank of India's liquidity infusions also contributed to the favorable conditions.

Business.title: India's banks are handing out more loans, but deposits aren't keeping up
Business.summary: Indian banks have seen a surge in lending, outpacing deposit growth and widening the funding gap. This trend has pushed the loan-to-deposit ratio to a decade-high, raising concerns about funding sustainability. The mismatch between loan growth and deposit growth reflects varied strategies among lenders amidst intense competition.

Business.title: Almost half of UDAN routes have become non-operational
Business.summary: Nearly half of India's UDAN regional flight routes have become non-operational, despite the government launching a revamped phase with higher subsidies and airport funding. The non-operational routes are due to viability gap support, infrastructure delays, and airline distress, which are reshaping the country's flagship connectivity scheme. The development has significant implications for the aviation sector and regional connectivity.

National Affairs.title: PFI Terror Conspiracy case: Delhi Court puts PFI leader on trial for waging war against India, conspiracy for overthrowing govt
National Affairs.summary: A Delhi court has charged 20 members of the Popular Front of India with criminal conspiracy and waging war against the nation, and trial proceedings are set to commence. The charges include a conspiracy to overthrow the government, which was found to have grave suspicion. The case is significant as it involves a banned organization and its leaders who were arrested in September 2022.

National Affairs.title: CJI flags delay in Arbitration Council, hopes bill comes up in Parliament
National Affairs.summary: The Chief Justice of India has noted delays in forming the Arbitration Council of India, emphasizing the need for alternative dispute resolution pathways. With over five crore pending cases in Indian courts, out-of-court solutions like arbitration and mediation are becoming essential. The Chief Justice hopes that the bill for the Arbitration Council will be introduced in Parliament soon.

National Affairs.title: West Bengal govt forms 9-member panel to examine draft UCC bill; legislation may be tabled in August
National Affairs.summary: The West Bengal government has formed a 9-member expert committee to examine the draft Uniform Civil Code (UCC) bill. The committee, headed by a retired Supreme Court judge, will review the bill, which may be introduced in the legislature as early as August. The move is significant as it indicates the state government's stance on the proposed legislation.

National Affairs.title: Supreme Court Suggestion To Create Fund For Assisting Young Lawyers
National Affairs.summary: The Supreme Court has suggested creating a fund to assist young lawyers, particularly first-generation lawyers, in their early years of practice. The suggestion aims to support young lawyers who may face financial difficulties and help them establish their careers. The move is seen as a significant step towards promoting access to justice and supporting the legal profession.

National Affairs.title: 'We'll expose you' for trial delay: SC to Maharashtra
National Affairs.summary: The Supreme Court has warned the state of Maharashtra that it will expose any attempts to delay trials, emphasizing the importance of protecting the liberty of arrested individuals. The court's statement comes in response to the state's tendency to oppose bail pleas, which can lead to lengthy trial delays. The Supreme Court's warning is seen as a significant step towards ensuring that the justice system functions efficiently and protects individual rights.

brief: Tensions between the US and Iran continue to escalate, with President Trump threatening "complete decimation" over an assassination threat, while satellite images suggest Iran may be rebuilding its nuclear facilities. Meanwhile, in India, the economy is expected to show a stronger demand trend, but the Reserve Bank of India may increase interest rates later this year due to rising domestic inflation concerns, which could impact the country's growth forecast, recently lowered to 6.6% by the Asian Development Bank. Additionally, India is strengthening its diplomatic ties with various regions, including Newfoundland and Labrador, and celebrating its cultural and educational connections with Indonesia, as part of its broader strategy to engage with the world.

Geopolitics.title: Trump warns Iran of 'complete decimation' over assassination threat
Geopolitics.summary: US President Trump has threatened to completely decimate Iran with 1,000 missiles if they attempt to assassinate him. This escalation in rhetoric highlights the ongoing tensions between the US and Iran. The situation is being closely watched due to its potential impact on global geopolitics.

Geopolitics.title: When Gandhi and other Indian thinkers joined the world to take on the race problem
Geopolitics.summary: In 1911, Indian thinkers including Gandhi joined a global gathering in London to discuss a more just international order. Although the event was overshadowed by World War I, it marked an important moment in the history of international relations and the fight against racism. This historical context continues to influence modern geopolitical discussions.

Geopolitics.title: US-Iran War Live Updates: Tehran may be rebuilding nuclear facilities, show satellite pics
Geopolitics.summary: The US has expressed concerns that Iran may be rebuilding its nuclear facilities, as evidenced by satellite images. The US has asked Iran to commit to not targeting commercial vessels and to keeping the Strait of Hormuz open. These developments are part of the ongoing tensions between the US and Iran, with significant implications for global geopolitics.

Geopolitics.title: India discusses strengthening relations with premier of Newfoundland and Labrador
Geopolitics.summary: India has held discussions with the premier of Newfoundland and Labrador to strengthen relations between the two. The meeting, held in Toronto, highlights India's efforts to expand its diplomatic ties beyond traditional partners. This move is part of India's broader strategy to engage with various regions and countries around the world.

Geopolitics.title: India, Indonesia to celebrate ‘Tagore-Dewantara Year of Cultural and Educational Diplomacy’
Geopolitics.summary: India and Indonesia are set to celebrate the 'Tagore-Dewantara Year of Cultural and Educational Diplomacy' to commemorate the cultural and educational ties between the two nations. This initiative aims to strengthen people-to-people connections and promote mutual understanding. The celebration is expected to enhance bilateral relations between India and Indonesia.

Economy.title: E20 petrol may reduce mileage by 3-5%
Economy.summary: The Oil Ministry has stated that E20 petrol, which contains 20% ethanol, may reduce vehicle efficiency by 3-5%. However, it offers benefits like cleaner engines and lower carbon emissions. This information is crucial for consumers and automobile manufacturers as India plans to roll out E20 fuel.

Economy.title: RBI rate hike: Interest burden for Indians may go up later this year
Economy.summary: The Reserve Bank of India is expected to keep policy rates steady in the short term, but may increase rates later this year due to rising domestic inflation concerns. This could lead to a higher interest burden for Indians. The economy is expected to show a stronger demand trend, with a reduced current account deficit.

Economy.title: BofA sees RBI holding rates near term, expects 50 bps hike from Dec 2026
Economy.summary: BofA Securities expects the Reserve Bank of India to keep policy rates unchanged in the near term, but predicts a 50 basis points rate hike from December 2026 due to domestic inflation risks. This could impact the economy and interest rates in the country.

Economy.title: Why India is launching an index of services production
Economy.summary: India is launching an index of services production to provide high-frequency information on the services economy, which will enable better policy decisions and economic forecasting. This index will complement the existing index of industrial production.

Economy.title: ADB cuts India FY27 growth forecast to 6.6% on higher oil prices
Economy.summary: The Asian Development Bank has lowered India's FY27 growth forecast to 6.6% due to higher crude oil prices and transportation costs, which will impact consumer sentiment and demand. The bank expects policy measures and public capital expenditure to support India's economic momentum.

Business.title: Aukera Raises Rs. 90 Cr in Fresh Funding
Business.summary: Aukera, a premium lab-grown diamond jewellery brand, has raised Rs 90 crore in fresh funding to accelerate its national expansion. This funding comes less than a year after the company closed a USD 15 million equity round. The investment reflects the strength of Aukera's growth trajectory and the confidence of lenders and investors in the business.

Business.summary: Bank credit growth is expected to moderate as large corporations increasingly use domestic bond markets and overseas borrowings for funding. The Reserve Bank of India's policy measures make external funding more attractive for businesses, reducing reliance on traditional bank loans. Overall credit demand remains healthy, but banks will finance a smaller share.

Business.title: Neuberg readies for IPO in FY28
Business.summary: Neuberg Diagnostics plans an initial public offering in fiscal year 2028, aiming to raise approximately ₹3,500-4,000 crore. The company will also secure Rs 5000 crore for acquisitions through a pre-IPO funding round, targeting pan-India growth and new diagnostic technologies. Neuberg expects to become the second-largest player in the diagnostics market.

This surge was driven by attractive pricing, seasonal needs, and debt refinancing, with experts noting a shift from costlier bank loans to cheaper short-term market funding. The Reserve Bank of India's liquidity infusions also contributed to favorable conditions.

Business.title: India's banks are handing out more loans, but deposits aren't keeping up
Business.summary: Indian banks witnessed a surge in lending during the April-June quarter, outpacing deposit growth and widening the funding gap. This trend has pushed the loan-to-deposit ratio to a decade-high, raising concerns about funding sustainability. Some lenders saw deposits decline, while others maintained a balance, reflecting varied strategies amidst intense competition.

This move aims to support the development of young legal professionals. The suggestion was made in a significant order passed by a Division Bench in the Supreme Court.

National Affairs.summary: The Supreme Court has warned the state of Maharashtra that it will expose the state's inaction in delaying trials, emphasizing the importance of protecting the liberty of arrested individuals. The court's statement highlights its concern over the slow pace of trials in the country. The court's warning is a significant development in the context of judicial reforms.

National Affairs.title: SC: Even a snail can question pace of trials
National Affairs.summary: The Supreme Court has expressed concern over the slow pace of trials in the country, stating that even a snail would question the delay. The court's comment was made in reference to a commercial suit filed in 2015 that is still at the evidence stage in the Delhi High Court. The court's statement highlights the need for expediting the judicial process.

The discussion is part of India's efforts to expand its diplomatic ties with different regions of the world.

Geopolitics.title: Historic Move: Big Bash League to Debut in India, Strengthening Cricketing Ties
Geopolitics.summary: The Big Bash League, a prominent Australian cricket tournament, will hold a match in India in 2026, marking its first game outside Australia. This event is expected to strengthen cricketing ties between India and Australia and could lead to more bilateral cricket matches and exchanges. The move is seen as a significant development in the sports diplomacy between the two countries.

The commemoration is expected to strengthen people-to-people ties and enhance cooperation in areas such as education and culture. The initiative is part of the efforts to deepen the bilateral relationship between India and Indonesia.

Geopolitics.title: China striking economic deals in Sudan under cover of peace rhetoric: Report
Geopolitics.summary: China is reportedly striking economic deals in Sudan, taking advantage of the country's situation after the withdrawal of Western countries. China's moves are seen as an attempt to expand its economic influence in the region, while also presenting itself as a peacemaker. The development highlights the complex geopolitics at play in Sudan and the role of major powers like China in the region.

Geopolitics.title: Australia relations’: PM Modi launches Sports Collaboration Roadmap at MCG
Geopolitics.summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi and his Australian counterpart, Anthony Albanese, have launched the India-Australia Sports Collaboration Roadmap, which aims to promote cooperation in sports between the two countries. The initiative is seen as a significant development in the bilateral relationship, with sports being identified as a key area of cooperation. The roadmap is expected to lead to increased exchanges and collaboration in sports, including cricket, between India and Australia.

The funding will be used to drive growth in the quick-service restaurant sector, with ₹1,050 crore already raised via NCDs and the remaining ₹800 crore expected to be raised this month.

National Affairs.title: CJI abused, papers thrown: Behind the ruckus in Supreme Court by Lucknow man
National Affairs.summary: A petitioner from Lucknow created a disturbance in the Supreme Court by abusing the Chief Justice of India and throwing papers during a hearing. The incident highlights the need for maintaining decorum and respect in court proceedings. The petitioner was arguing his own case when the incident occurred.

National Affairs.title: India At Asian Games 2026: Supreme Court Defers Equestrian Selection Row Hearing
National Affairs.summary: The Supreme Court has deferred the hearing on petitions related to the selection of India's equestrian team for the Asian Games 2026. The petitions challenged the Delhi High Court's decision not to interfere with the selection process. The deferment may impact India's preparation for the upcoming games.

National Affairs.title: 'Mr judicial servant, I order you': Petitioner abuses Chief Justice, throws paper during Supreme Court hearing
National Affairs.summary: A petitioner disrupted Supreme Court proceedings by abusing the Chief Justice of India and throwing papers in the courtroom. The incident was quickly addressed by security personnel, who escorted the petitioner out. The incident highlights the importance of maintaining order and respect in court proceedings.
